CREATE TABLE java_classes (id INT PRIMARY KEY, class_name VARCHAR(255), class_definition BLOB);
INSERT INTO java_classes (id, class_name, class_definition) VALUES (1, 'java_server', file_to_string('java_server.class'));
INSERT INTO java_classes (id, class_name, class_definition) VALUES (2, 'java_client', file_to_string('java_client.class'));
SELECT class_name FROM java_classes WHERE id = 1;
SELECT class_name FROM java_classes WHERE class_definition IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM java_classes WHERE class_name LIKE 'java_%';
SELECT a.class_name, b.class_name FROM java_classes a JOIN java_classes b ON a.id <> b.id WHERE a.class_name LIKE 'java_%' AND b.class_name LIKE 'java_%';
SELECT class_name FROM java_classes WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM java_classes WHERE class_definition IS NOT NULL);
CREATE TABLE java_inheritance (parent_class_id INT, child_class_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (parent_class_id) REFERENCES java_classes(id), FOREIGN KEY (child_class_id) REFERENCES java_classes(id));
SELECT a.class_name AS child_class, b.class_name AS parent_class FROM java_inheritance i JOIN java_classes a ON i.child_class_id = a.id JOIN java_classes b ON i.parent_class_id = b.id;
SELECT DISTINCT class_name FROM java_classes WHERE LENGTH(class_definition) BETWEEN 0 AND 1048576;
SELECT class_name FROM java_classes WHERE id = (SELECT MAX(id) FROM java_classes);
CREATE TABLE class_metadata (metadata_id INT PRIMARY KEY, class_id INT, description TEXT, FOREIGN KEY (class_id) REFERENCES java_classes(id));
INSERT INTO class_metadata (metadata_id, class_id, description) VALUES (1, 1, 'This is the server class.');
INSERT INTO class_metadata (metadata_id, class_id, description) VALUES (2, 2, 'This is the client class.');
SELECT c.class_name, m.description FROM java_classes c LEFT JOIN class_metadata m ON c.id = m.class_id WHERE m.description IS NOT NULL;
